things.name: Belgrade Fortress & Kalemegdan Park
things.description: Massive 2,000-year-old fortress at the confluence of the Sava and Danube rivers with sweeping views

things.name: Skadarlija Bohemian Quarter
things.description: Cobblestone 19th-century artist quarter with traditional kafanas, live music and Serbian cuisine

things.name: Saint Sava Temple
things.description: One of the world's largest Orthodox churches with a glittering Byzantine mosaic interior

things.name: Nikola Tesla Museum
things.description: Unique museum dedicated to the inventor genius, housing his ashes and original AC motor

things.name: Ada Ciganlija River Beach
things.description: Artificial island on the Sava known as Belgrade's Sea — beaches, sports and summer culture

things.name: National Museum of Serbia
things.description: The country's largest museum with Serbian medieval art, the Miroslav Gospel and Roman artefacts

things.name: Zemun Old Town
things.description: Former Austro-Hungarian town on the Danube with the Gardos Tower and excellent fish restaurants

things.name: Knez Mihailova Street
things.description: The main pedestrian boulevard lined with 19th-century buildings, street performers and shops

things.name: Museum of Yugoslav History
things.description: Tito's tomb and fascinating museum on Yugoslavia with the Blue Train and gifts from world leaders

things.name: Novi Sad Day Trip
things.description: Vibrant Danube city 90 km away with the Petrovaradin Fortress and the EXIT music festival site
